description: The Eastman Gang was a predominantly Jewish-American street gang that dominated parts of the New York City underworld from the late 1890s to the early 1910s. Founded by Monk Eastman, the gang operated in Manhattan's Lower East Side, engaging in armed robbery, theft, illegal gambling, extortion, prostitution, and opium peddling. It succeeded the Whyos as a prominent non-Irish gang and had strong political connections through Tammany Hall. The gang dissolved in the early 1910s.
